telemetryDetails.title: What minimal telemetry includes
telemetryDetails.intro: Telemetry is intentionally minimal and excludes file names and file contents. Only a small set of technical diagnostics is sent:

items.instanceId: A random instance identifier (a one-time generated GUID, not tied to you)
items.serverUrl: Public server URL, so we know whether the cloud is reachable

telemetryDetails.outro: That's everything. No file contents, file names, usernames, or emails are sent in telemetry payloads. Standard network metadata (such as source IP) may still exist at the transport level.

setup.title: Server setup wizard
setup.subtitle: Complete the initial server configuration to get started. This won't take long and will personalize the app for you.

errors.saveChoiceFailed: Something went wrong. Try choosing another option.

setup.intro: You'll be asked simple questions about your Cotton Cloud usage preferences. If you're not sure, don't worry - many settings can be changed later.
setup.onlyAdminCanSetup: Only administrators can configure the application. Please ask an administrator to perform the initial setup.

questions.requiresTooltip: To enable this option, you need to select:

multiuser.title: You plan to use it:
multiuser.subtitle: This affects storage optimization and some security settings.
multiuser.linkAria: Open help in a new tab

descriptions.family: File deduplication, shared search index, and pre-installed modules.
descriptions.many: Stricter mode for security between unknown users.
descriptions.unknown: Will set the best optimal defaults.

usage.title: You plan to use the cloud for:
usage.subtitle: Select one or more options

descriptions.photos: Will install AI search and text recognition add-ons.
descriptions.documents: Optimization for office files and PDFs for home archive
descriptions.media: Optimization for video files and streaming, integration with movie database.
descriptions.other: General settings for mixed file types.

storage.title: Where do you want to store your data?
storage.subtitle: Choose storage location for your files. Cotton Cloud will use server disk for hot cache regardless of your choice.

descriptions.local: Store files on the server's local filesystem.
descriptions.s3: Store files in remote S3-compatible object storage.

email.title: Email configuration
email.subtitle: Choose where email notifications will be sent from.

descriptions.cloud: Cotton Bridge will send notification emails for this instance. Requires telemetry to be enabled.
descriptions.custom: Configure your own SMTP server for sending email notifications.
descriptions.none: The simplest option, but you won't even be able to recover your password.

telemetry.title: Allow sending statistics?
telemetry.subtitle: This data helps us improve the product and is intentionally minimal. It's one of the few ways to support the project with automatic feedback, and helps us understand how Cotton Cloud performs on different systems and configurations.

descriptions.allow: Send minimal diagnostics used for software feedback and bug fixes.

telemetry.disabledTooltip: To disable telemetry, you need to turn off the following services:

geoIpLookup.title: Resolve IP addresses?
geoIpLookup.subtitle: Cotton can turn IP addresses into city and country names. This can be used, for example, in security notifications to show where a sign-in came from and help detect unusual access.

descriptions.disabled: No location lookup will be made. Features that can show sign-in location will keep raw IP addresses only.
descriptions.cottonCloud: Resolve IP addresses through Cotton Bridge. Requires telemetry to be enabled.
descriptions.local: Resolve IP addresses on your server with a local GeoIP Light database/package.
descriptions.custom: Paste a URL to your own service. Cotton will try to extract location-related fields from JSON automatically.

customGeoIpLookupUrl.title: Custom IP resolver
customGeoIpLookupUrl.subtitle: Enter an HTTP endpoint that returns country, region, and city as JSON.

ai.title: AI and Computations
ai.subtitle: Choose where AI computations will be performed for search, recognition, and other features.

descriptions.local: Slow, most heavy features disabled and model quality is simplified. But the quality is still great - more than enough for any tasks.
descriptions.runner: Connect a separate GPU runner for computations. Fast, high quality - best models and support for all features.
descriptions.cloud: Cotton Bridge can run AI computations for this instance. Requires internet connection and may have limits.

timezone.title: Time Zone
timezone.subtitle: Select your time zone to avoid being disturbed by notifications and disk noise at night. This is only for server routines, Web UI will always show times in your local time zone.

general.title: General settings
general.description: Core server behavior and notification location lookup.

validation.publicBaseUrlInvalid: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS base URL without credentials, query, or hash.
validation.publicBaseUrlMismatch: Current address is {{current}}, but generated public links will use {{configured}}. Be careful before saving this value.
validation.timezoneInvalid: Choose a valid IANA time zone from the list.
validation.customGeoIpLookupUrlInvalid: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS lookup URL.

help.publicBaseUrl: Despite this setting, Cotton determines the public URL automatically almost everywhere. Set it only when automatic detection is wrong.
help.timezone: This helps us avoid running background jobs while you sleep, so your disks do not grind at night.
help.telemetry: Cotton collects minimal technical diagnostics so the developer can see errors and fix them, because not everyone reports issues. Enabling telemetry also unlocks Cotton Bridge services out of the box.
help.allowDeduplication: Controls whether one user can learn that another user already stores the same file. When disabled, identical data is still deduplicated on the server after upload, but it is needlessly transferred first. When enabled, another user may learn that someone in this cloud uploaded that file before. This is safe for family and trusted friends.
help.allowGlobalIndexing: Allows the server to decrypt user files and build a deep search index. Without it, search only uses file and folder names.
help.serverUsage: Optimizes feature recommendations and a few settings. It is not a critical parameter, so choose whatever feels right.

storageSpaceHelp.open: What does storage space mode affect?
storageSpaceHelp.title: Storage space mode
storageSpaceHelp.description: This controls how aggressively Cotton uses and frees storage: garbage collection timing, cleanup of unreferenced data, cache/data reuse, and how many old file versions can remain available. Every time a file is updated, Cotton keeps a shadow copy by retaining a reference to the previous file data until cleanup removes it. Unlimited keeps data longer and cleans mostly when space is needed; Space Saver frees storage sooner. Balanced stays between those two.

computionMode.inDevelopment: This feature is still in development.

geoIpLookupModeDescription.CottonCloud: Resolve IP addresses through Cotton Bridge. This can be used by features such as security notifications.
geoIpLookupModeDescription.MaxMindLocal: Resolve IP addresses locally with a GeoIP Light database/package.
geoIpLookupModeDescription.CustomHttp: Paste a URL to your own service. Cotton will try to extract location-related fields from JSON automatically.

state.restoreIfEmptyHint: If the database is lost and you want it to restore automatically, set COTTON_RESTORE_DATABASE_IF_EMPTY=true in the container environment. Database must be empty without any tables for this to work.

storageStatistics.title: Storage statistics

state.empty: No scheduled GC buckets in the selected period.

securityDiagnostics.title: Security checkup
securityDiagnostics.description: Server-side checks for account hygiene, master-key handling, and process-memory exposure.

strong.title: Strong posture
strong.summary: This instance has the expected production hardening. It is a good fit for public exposure when you also trust the host and backups.

good.title: Good self-hosted baseline
good.summary: This is good for a family or small team. The remaining items are mostly operational hardening and account hygiene.

home.title: Home-use baseline
home.summary: This is reasonable for home and daily use, but do not treat the host as hostile. A server compromise may still expose keys or admin access.

exposed.title: Practical attack paths remain
exposed.summary: The instance has issues that a local attacker, compromised container, or stolen admin password could realistically use.

unsafe.title: Unsafe for sensitive data
unsafe.summary: Fix the critical warnings before storing valuable private data. At this level, the master key or admin control may be too easy to reach.

risks.empty: No warnings were detected by the current server-side checks.

threatVectors.public-instance: Internet visitors can create accounts and store content. Quotas, default demo data, and abuse monitoring become part of the security boundary.
threatVectors.master-key-from-environment: If deployment metadata, shell history, or container inspection exposes environment variables, the master key may sit close to the encrypted data it protects.
threatVectors.admins-without-2fa: An admin password becomes the only factor. Password reuse, phishing, or leaked browser sessions can turn into full instance control.
threatVectors.dotnet-diagnostics-enabled: A local process with enough rights can ask the .NET runtime for dumps or traces, and those dumps may contain live secrets from memory.
threatVectors.process-dumpable: A same-UID process or ptrace-capable debugger can dump the server memory and search for the in-memory master key.
threatVectors.sys-ptrace-capability: A debug or privileged container can inspect and alter process memory. That is exactly the capability memory-only keys try to avoid.
threatVectors.new-privileges-allowed: If compromised code finds a setuid helper or capability-bearing binary, it has a wider path to gain privileges inside the container.
threatVectors.seccomp-disabled: The kernel syscall surface is wider than Docker's normal production baseline, so a container escape bug gets more room to breathe.
threatVectors.running-as-root: Remote code execution would run as root inside the container, which increases damage against mounted volumes and helper processes.
threatVectors.process-hardening-failed: Cotton requested process hardening, but the process still started without the expected dump or ptrace protection.
threatVectors.db-integrity-unsigned-rows: Unsigned protected rows can be modified directly in PostgreSQL without Cotton detecting the tampering at read boundaries.
threatVectors.db-integrity-bridge-mode: During the bridge window, missing signatures are tolerated so existing installations can be signed. Leaving it on permanently weakens the database tamper boundary.
threatVectors.root-filesystem-writable: A writable container root filesystem gives exploited code more places to drop tools, alter runtime files, or persist until the container is recreated.
threatVectors.docker-socket-mounted: The Docker socket lets code inside the web container create privileged containers, mount host paths, and take over the host.
threatVectors.host-pid-namespace: Sharing the host PID namespace exposes host processes through procfs and makes process-memory attacks easier to aim.
threatVectors.mandatory-access-control-unconfined: Without an enforcing AppArmor or SELinux profile, a container escape or kernel-facing bug has fewer policy boundaries to cross.
threatVectors.core-dumps-enabled: Core dumps are memory snapshots. If Cotton crashes while secrets are resident, a dump can preserve the master key or decrypted configuration.

fixes.public-instance: If this instance is not meant to be public, disable public/demo mode (unset COTTON_PUBLIC_INSTANCE) and restart.
fixes.master-key-from-environment: Prefer unlocking via the /unlock page instead of COTTON_MASTER_KEY, or make sure the variable is not retained in deployment metadata or exposed to exec sessions.
fixes.admins-without-2fa: Enable two-factor authentication (TOTP) for every admin account from Profile → Security.
fixes.dotnet-diagnostics-enabled: Set DOTNET_EnableDiagnostics=0 (and COMPlus_EnableDiagnostics=0) in the container environment and restart.
fixes.process-dumpable: Set COTTON_PROCESS_HARDENING=true (or use the official image defaults) so the process requests PR_SET_DUMPABLE=0 at startup.
fixes.sys-ptrace-capability: Drop the CAP_SYS_PTRACE capability from the container (for example cap_drop: [ALL]).
fixes.new-privileges-allowed: Run the container with no-new-privileges:true so the process cannot gain privileges through setuid binaries.
fixes.seccomp-disabled: Run with a seccomp profile enabled (avoid seccomp=unconfined) to restrict the syscalls available to the process.
fixes.running-as-root: Run the container as a non-root user; the official image runs as the unprivileged app user by default.
fixes.process-hardening-failed: Process hardening was requested but could not be applied — check the container capabilities/permissions and the startup logs.
fixes.db-integrity-unsigned-rows: Let the database-integrity bridge backfill finish signing legacy rows (keep the instance running), then disable bridge mode.
fixes.db-integrity-bridge-mode: Bridge mode is a transitional database-integrity migration state managed by Cotton itself. It is finalized by upgrading to the Cotton release that completes the integrity migration — there is no operator action to take.
fixes.root-filesystem-writable: Run the container with a read-only root filesystem (read_only: true) and mount only the storage volume as writable.
fixes.docker-socket-mounted: Do not mount the Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) into the container.
fixes.host-pid-namespace: Do not share the host PID namespace (avoid pid: host) so the process cannot observe other host processes.
fixes.mandatory-access-control-unconfined: Apply an AppArmor or SELinux profile to confine the process instead of running unconfined.
fixes.core-dumps-enabled: Disable core dumps for the container (ulimits: core: 0) so secrets cannot leak into dump files.

passed.master-key-from-environment: Master key not exposed via the environment

aesAcceleration.title: Hardware-accelerated encryption
aesAcceleration.body: This processor exposes AES-NI / PCLMULQDQ instructions, so AES-GCM runs on dedicated hardware paths — encryption stays fast and adds little CPU overhead.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Manage access groups separately from individual users.

identityProviders.title: Identity providers
identityProviders.description: Configure OpenID Connect providers for sign-in and account linking.

fields.name: Name
fields.allowAccountCreation: Allow this provider to create new accounts
fields.requireVerifiedEmail: Require verified email for account creation
fields.syncAvatar: Import provider avatar when the user has no avatar

help.slug: Leave empty to generate it from the provider name. Existing slugs stay stable.
help.redirectUrl: Register this exact callback URL in the provider application.
help.clientSecret: Leave empty if this provider uses a public client.
help.clientSecretConfigured: A secret is already saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
help.scopes: Separate scopes with spaces or commas. openid is always required.
help.allowedEmailDomains: Optional comma-separated domains for automatic account creation.

delete.title: Delete {{name}}?
delete.description: Existing links for this provider will stop working. Users can keep signing in with other methods.

resetPassword.title: Reset password
resetPassword.success: Password has been reset successfully. You can now sign in with your new password.

errors.invalidToken: The reset link is invalid or has expired.
errors.failed: Failed to reset password. Please try again.

verifyEmail.title: Email verification
verifyEmail.success: Your email has been verified successfully!

errors.invalidToken: The verification link is invalid or has expired.
errors.failed: Failed to verify email. Please try again.

access.title: This application will be able to:

bullets.files: Access your Cotton Cloud files and folders.
bullets.content: Upload, download, rename, move, and delete content.
bullets.session: Stay signed in until you revoke the application session.

approved.title: Access granted
approved.message: You can return to the application.

denied.title: Request denied
denied.message: The application was not signed in.

errors.unavailable: This sign-in request is no longer available.

unlock.title: Unlock Cotton
unlock.caption: The server is waiting for the master key before starting the app.

checking.title: Checking lock state
checking.caption: Cotton is preparing the secure startup flow.

unlock.bootstrapRequired: First unlock requires the bootstrap token from the server logs.
unlock.bootstrapRequiredWithExpiry: First unlock requires the bootstrap token from the server logs. It expires at {{expiresAt}}.
unlock.generated: Generated. Store this key before unlocking.
unlock.unlocked: Master key accepted. Cotton is starting.
